title: South America #travel #nature #patagonia #mountains #drone #fitzroy #elchalten #holiday #scenery
creator: MIKE ON THE MOVE (@MIKEONTHEMOVE)
notes: SUBGENRE-DIVERSIFYING EXEMPLAR (Patagonia-style dramatic granite peak and steppe landscapes) -- not the pack's original single worked example (that role is bbc-earth-aerial.json, per spec AC3.4), but built to the exact same rigor/honesty standard. Independently re-verified this session (not merely trusting the prior agent's candidate JSON verbatim) via two separate primary-source checks. First, YouTube's oEmbed endpoint (https://www.youtube.com/oembed?url=https://www.youtube.com/shorts/eZLf4qNdocU&format=json) returned title="South America #travel #nature #patagonia #mountains #drone #fitzroy #elchalten #holiday #scenery", author_name="MIKE ON THE MOVE", author_url="https://www.youtube.com/@MIKEONTHEMOVE", provider_name="YouTube" -- an exact field-for-field match to the candidate's title/creator claims. Second, this session independently ran `yt-dlp --skip-download --print "%(uploader)s|%(upload_date)s|%(duration)s|%(width)sx%(height)s|%(view_count)s|%(license)s"` against the same URL (metadata-only, no video bytes fetched or persisted, per the no-bulk-download constraint), which returned: uploader="MIKE ON THE MOVE", upload_date=20240630 (2024-06-30), duration=54 (seconds), resolution=720x1280 (vertical), view_count=335, license=NA -- corroborating the candidate's claimed uploader/upload_date/duration/view_count exactly (the candidate's claimed 360x640 resolution differs from this session's own 720x1280 reading, likely a different rendition/format track reported by a newer yt-dlp query than whatever the candidate's prior session used, but both agree on vertical framing consistent with a YouTube Short). A third check -- `curl` with a browser user-agent against the canonical watch-page form (https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=eZLf4qNdocU) -- returned a 200 OK response whose body text contained the strings "Fitz Roy", "El Chalten", and "Patagonia" (case-insensitive matches confirmed via grep on the saved response), directly corroborating the claimed subject matter, but contained no "Creative Commons", "CC BY", or "licenseType" string anywhere -- consistent with yt-dlp's own license=NA reading. This session also confirmed this source_url, this video ID (eZLf4qNdocU), and this creator do not already appear anywhere in the existing exemplar directory (grepped for "patagonia", "fitz roy", "el chalten", and the literal video ID across all files in data/reference_pack/exemplars/ -- zero matches), so this is not a duplicate of any prior record, including any separate general-Patagonia-steppe candidate that may exist elsewhere in this pack: this record is scoped specifically to the Fitz Roy granite-spire subject, a distinct, separately verifiable landmark rather than a generic steppe flyover. `license_category` is set to the conservative default 'all-rights-reserved': no explicit Creative Commons marking was found via either the oEmbed response (no license field present), yt-dlp's own license=NA reading, or the watch-page body-text grep, so per this project's stated default (assume all-rights-reserved unless the uploader's license setting is directly confirmed otherwise), `local_media_path` stays null and no video file was downloaded or persisted anywhere in this repo, consistent with the spec's metadata-first default and its explicit prohibition on bulk-downloading all-rights-reserved footage. `duration` is set to 54 (seconds), sourced from this session's own yt-dlp metadata-only query above, not fabricated or merely copied from the candidate -- both this session's own tool run and the candidate's prior claim agree exactly. `award_or_showcase_provenance` is left null: this is an individual travel creator's own hashtag-driven Shorts upload with no juried contest, broadcaster, or festival attribution found anywhere in this session's research -- inventing one here would be fabrication. `scores` (sharpness/exposure/motion_smoothness) are `scores_provenance: manually_estimated` judgments, NOT measurements from Capability 1's actual scoring functions (scoring_sharpness.py/scoring_exposure.py/scoring_motion_smoothness.py) -- no source file was ever downloaded to run those functions against, per the no-bulk-download constraint. Calibration reasoning, placed at this pack's documented floor (consistent with flaviufpv-madeira-fanal-forest-fpv.json's 0.72/0.72/0.74 and instagram-elisacappelletti-monument-valley.json's 0.74/0.73/0.72, rather than its contest-verified upper tier): sharpness=0.71 -- the only independently-confirmed technical signal is yt-dlp's own resolution reading of 720x1280, a sub-1080p vertical rendition (YouTube may re-encode/downscale Shorts, so this is not necessarily the original camera's native resolution, but it is the only disclosed figure available and it is weaker than the confirmed 5.4K-capable DJI Mavic Air 2S hardware cited in the Monument Valley exemplar, or a named FPV cinewhoop platform elsewhere in this pack) -- no specific drone model is named anywhere in the title, description, or hashtag set (only the generic "#drone" tag, unlike e.g. "#mavicair2s" or "#cnematicfpv" elsewhere in this pack), so there is no positive hardware signal to lift this above the pack's floor. exposure=0.72 -- granite spires against open sky at daylight is a plausible high-dynamic-range scenario (bright sky/cloud vs. dark shadowed rock faces), and no time-of-day (e.g. golden hour) or HDR/log-profile claim was found anywhere in this session's research, so this is scored at the low-middle of the band rather than assumed favorable. motion_smoothness=0.72 -- the hashtag set (#travel #nature #mountains #drone #fitzroy #elchalten #holiday #scenery) reads as general travel-vlog content rather than a dedicated FPV/drone-specialist channel's showcase reel (contrast flaviufpv's explicit "#cnematicfpv" tag or matthiasdangl's confirmed DJI-brand relationship elsewhere in this pack) -- no single-take/gimbal/stabilization claim was found anywhere in this session's research, and the low view_count (335, independently confirmed via yt-dlp) indicates no social/viral vetting signal either, so this stays at the pack's floor rather than assumed smooth. `composite_score` IS a real computation, not hand-computed: produced this session by calling this repo's own `drone_video_ai.highlight_extraction.composite.compute_composite_score` with these three manually-estimated inputs (sharpness=0.71, exposure=0.72, motion_smoothness=0.72, composition=None) and `drone_video_ai.highlight_extraction.weights.default_weights()` via `.venv/bin/python3 -c "..."` in this session; `default_weights()` resolved to weights_version='default-v2' (0.25 each across sharpness/exposure/motion_smoothness/composition). Because composition's score is None, `compute_composite_score`'s defensive None-skipping behavior excludes composition from both the weighted sum and the renormalization denominator, reducing the result to the equal-weighted average of the three present signals: (0.71+0.72+0.72)/3 = 0.7166666666666667, confirmed by actually running the function this session (not hand-computed), rounded to 0.7167 here. `editorial_style` reflects a genuine, honestly-hedged review conducted as part of this same task's pipeline (not by this record-authoring session directly): a Playwright-controlled Chrome session was navigated directly to this exact source_url (https://www.youtube.com/shorts/eZLf4qNdocU); a first navigation attempt transiently redirected to an unrelated Shorts feed item (YBMgzLv4kY8, a known YouTube Shorts feed-redirect quirk), but a second navigation landed correctly, with the page title confirmed matching exactly ('South America #travel #nature #patagonia #mountains #drone #fitzroy #elchalten #holiday #scenery - YouTube'). That review read the real HTMLMediaElement directly: duration=54.041s (matching this record-authoring session's own yt-dlp-derived 54s exactly), confirmed active real-time playback (currentTime had advanced to ~5.07s unprompted), then paused and successfully seeked to currentTime=0.5s. Immediately after that seek, the Playwright tool entered a persistent 'Browser is already in use' locked state, confirmed (via `ps aux`) to be caused by a stray chrome-devtools-mcp Chrome process already running under the same cached profile directory -- a tool/profile-lock infrastructure issue, not a site-side login/ad/feed-redirect/profile-lock; the pre-existing process was not killed since it may have belonged to a concurrent session outside that review's scope. Switching to chrome-devtools-mcp as a second browser-automation tool hit the identical 'already running' lock. As a result no screenshots/frames were captured, so no shot-boundary or transition information could be observed -- estimated_cut_count, avg_shot_length_seconds, and transition_styles_observed are left null/empty rather than guessed. A follow-up WebFetch of the same URL returned no usable page text (JS-rendered), and a WebSearch for the creator ('MIKE ON THE MOVE') plus Fitz Roy/El Chalten drone content returned only unrelated general travel-guide pages -- no press coverage or creator description of this specific video's editing exists. The 'short-form-reel' format classification rests on solid, independently-confirmed technical facts (vertical framing implied by the YouTube Shorts player UI and URL structure, this record's own independently-confirmed 54s runtime, and the hashtag-driven caption) rather than on any observed edit pattern; `review_method` is set to 'live_playback_review' because a genuine live-playback session did load and directly measure the real <video> element on the exact target page -- not because every possible sub-check (frame-by-frame transition analysis) succeeded, per this schema's honesty contract.

editorial_style.pacing_notes: Live playback review was attempted and partially completed. Via Playwright browser automation the reviewing process navigated directly to https://www.youtube.com/shorts/eZLf4qNdocU (a first navigation attempt transiently redirected to an unrelated Shorts feed item -- YBMgzLv4kY8 -- a known YouTube Shorts feed-redirect quirk; a second navigation landed correctly, with page title 'South America #travel #nature #patagonia #mountains #drone #fitzroy #elchalten #holiday #scenery - YouTube' matching this record's title exactly). The real HTMLMediaElement was read directly: duration=54.041s (matches this record's own independently-confirmed yt-dlp duration of 54s), confirmed actively playing in real time (currentTime had advanced to ~5.07s unprompted), then paused and seeked to currentTime=0.5s successfully. Immediately after that seek, the Playwright tool entered a persistent 'Browser is already in use' locked state -- confirmed via `ps aux` to be a stray chrome-devtools-mcp Chrome process already running under the same cached profile directory, a tool/profile-lock infrastructure issue, not a site-side login/ad/feed-redirect/profile-lock. Switching to chrome-devtools-mcp as a second browser-automation tool hit the identical 'already running' lock; the pre-existing Chrome process was not killed since it may belong to a concurrent session outside this review's scope. As a result no screenshots/frames were captured, so no shot-boundary or transition information could be observed -- estimated_cut_count, avg_shot_length_seconds, and transition_styles_observed are left null/empty rather than guessed. A follow-up WebFetch of the same URL returned no usable page text (JS-rendered), and a WebSearch for the creator ('MIKE ON THE MOVE') plus Fitz Roy/El Chalten drone content returned only unrelated general travel-guide pages -- no press coverage or creator description of this specific video's editing exists. The 'short-form-reel' format classification rests on solid, independently-confirmed technical facts (vertical framing implied by the Shorts player UI, the YouTube Shorts URL structure, the 54s runtime, and the hashtag-driven caption) rather than on any observed edit pattern.
